prompt:
The city fleet-ops CI image moved to gcc 16 this week and the snowplow shift-report module stopped building. The flags haven't changed — we've always shipped -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror — but the old box's compiler apparently let all of this slide and the new one does not. Here's `make -k test` with the caret art trimmed:

    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c plow.c
    plow.c:3:53: error: unused parameter 'rounded' [-Werror=unused-parameter]
    plow.c:7:52: error: unused parameter 'shift_mask' [-Werror=unused-parameter]
    plow.c:13:23: error: comparison of integer expressions of different signedness: 'int' and 'size_t' [-Werror=sign-compare]
    plow.c:20:23: error: comparison of integer expressions of different signedness: 'int' and 'size_t' [-Werror=sign-compare]
    make: *** [Makefile:8: plow.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c report.c
    report.c:6:21: error: missing initializer for field 'salt_kg' of 'const struct PlowLeg' [-Werror=missing-field-initializers]
    report.c:9:40: error: format '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument 5 has type 'long int' [-Werror=format=]
    report.c:9:51: error: format '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument 6 has type 'size_t' [-Werror=format=]
    report.c:26:31: error: format '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument 5 has type 'long int' [-Werror=format=]
    report.c:26:42: error: format '%u' expects argument of type 'unsigned int', but argument 6 has type 'size_t' [-Werror=format=]
    make: *** [Makefile:11: report.o] Error 1
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_plow.c
    make: Target 'test' not remade because of errors.

test_plow.c itself compiles clean, so it's just the two modules. Nothing about the report is wrong at runtime as far as dispatch has ever noticed — the row format goes straight into the office spreadsheet import and it has looked right for two winters. So please make `make test` come out clean WITHOUT changing what any row prints or what the totals compute; the tests pin the rows byte-for-byte. Mind the filter signature too: filters are installed through the leg_filter typedef in plow.h, so their parameter list is not yours to slim down, even where a rule ignores the shift mask. Don't touch test_plow.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5W3 item 1: -Wall -Wextra warning sweep, all behavior-preserving. Authoring toolchain: gcc 16.1.1, -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror. Planted: (1) format mismatches x4 — %d for long (leg passes), %d for size_t (salt_kg), %d for long and %u for size_t in the totals row (probed: %ld-for-size_t is a sign-only same-width mismatch gcc's default -Wformat does NOT flag, so the planted wrong specifier is %u, which differs in width and errors reliably); fixed specifiers (%ld/%zu) produce byte-identical text for the tested values, and the tests pin rows byte-exact so a 'fix' that changes widths fails. (2) sign-compare: two `for (int i; i < n)` loops over size_t n in plow.c. (3) unused-parameter x2 with the catalog's void-cast-vs-removal decision: filter_any ignores shift_mask but its signature is pinned by the leg_filter typedef and a test that calls through the typedef (must (void)-cast or equivalent), while static km_for's dead `rounded` param has one internal call site and is naturally removed (either fix compiles; reference removes it). (4) missing-field-initializers on the file-scope TOTALS_ROW initializer ({"TOTALS", 0} missing salt_kg/lane_km); note gcc does NOT warn on plain {0}, so the test file's fully-initialized arrays and the fix are both clean. Prompt is the trimmed make -k dump per family-5W convention; it names files/lines (that is the genre) but every fix is the teacher's diagnosis. Behavior teeth: totals, strict-inequality refill boundary, typedef'd filter dispatch incl. NULL leg, fixed-width rows and full 4-row report byte-exact.
